title: Web App: Command Demos

actors.name: Web Visitor
actors.description: Browser user interacting with the live command demo section of the public web app

actors.name: Demo Registry (commandDemos)
actors.description: Static map of 16 command demo objects keyed by commandId; exposed via getCommandDemo(commandId)

actors.name: demo-renderer.js
actors.description: Generates HTML for command demos (split-screen or fallback) and initialises demo JS

actors.name: demo-toggles.js
actors.description: Attaches DOM event listeners for before/after toggles, relationship-tag navigation, and interactive buttons

use_cases.name: View Command Demo
use_cases.description: Visitor selects a command; renderCommandDemo(commandId) looks up the demo via getCommandDemo(), then returns split-screen HTML with .split-before / .split-after panels and a caption. Special static views are shown for 'impeccable' and 'shape'.

use_cases.name: Toggle Before / After State
use_cases.description: Visitor clicks a toggle switch (.demo-toggle-switch[data-demo]). setupCommandDemoToggles() attaches a click handler that calls handleCommandDemoToggle(demoId, isAfter). The handler extracts commandId (strips 'command-' prefix), retrieves the demo, updates viewport data-state, and either calls demo.onToggle(viewport, isAfter) or swaps innerHTML with demo.before / demo.after.

use_cases.name: Initialise Interactive Demo JS
use_cases.description: After demo HTML is injected into the DOM, initCommandDemo(commandId, container) is called. It fetches the demo and, if demo.init exists, locates the .split-after .split-content element and calls demo.init(demoArea). Enables command-specific JS behaviour (e.g., animate).
use_cases.condition: Only executes if demo has an init() function

use_cases.name: Navigate to Related Command
use_cases.description: Visitor clicks a .relationship-tag[data-command] element inside the command detail panel. setupCommandDemoToggles receives allCommands list and a selectCommand callback; the click handler finds the matching command object and calls selectCommand(command) to navigate.

use_cases.name: Interact with Demo Element
use_cases.description: Visitor clicks an interactive element within a demo (e.g., a like button .int-fb-active[data-action='like']). setupInteractiveButtons() (called inside setupDemoToggles) attaches a click handler that toggles the 'liked' CSS class and updates adjacent label text ('Liked!' / 'Click to try!').
use_cases.scope_note: Currently only 'like' action implemented; pattern is extensible

use_cases.name: Browse Command Demo Fallback
use_cases.description: For commandIds with no registered demo, renderCommandDemo returns a placeholder ('Visual demo coming soon'). For 'impeccable' and 'shape', rich static informational panels are returned instead of split-screen.

command_demo_object_shape.layout_caption: Equal spacing everywhere → Intentional rhythm and hierarchy

key_relationships: demo-renderer.js produces HTML consumed by demo-toggles.js event listeners
demos/commands/index.js aggregates 16 individual command demo modules
Each command demo module exports { id, before, after?, caption, init?, onToggle? }
important_notes: All toggle handlers guard against double-initialisation via toggle.dataset.initialized. Command demos use split-screen HTML (no toggle switch) while skill demos use before/after toggle switches — the two rendering paths are distinct. The selectCommand callback passed to setupCommandDemoToggles is defined externally (page-level controller), not inside these modules.
